title: Lifecycle Engine Integration with Outer Session Loop
consumer_purpose: Wire the lifecycle engine into the outer session loop via a compositor layer so that agents with lifecycle definitions get phase-gated tool access and phase transition management
claim_atoms: definition.ts has LifecycleDefinition, PhaseDefinition, TransitionDefinition schemas with Effect Schema
builtins.ts provides CARTOGRAPHER_LIFECYCLE, EXECUTOR_LIFECYCLE, CRITIC_LIFECYCLE, LANE_OWNER_LIFECYCLE constants + BUILTIN_LIFECYCLES record
engine.ts implements LifecycleEngine as an Effect Service with hook-based execute() supporting retry/escalation/phase transitions
loop.ts implements LifecycleLoop compositor that wraps SessionPrompt.loop, creating per-phase filtered ToolRegistry layers
No modifications to dirty files (prompt.ts, processor.ts, registry.ts)
content:
# Lifecycle Engine Integration Plan

## Files to create in packages/opencode/src/lifecycle/

### 1. definition.ts — Phase/LifecycleDefinition schemas
- LifecycleType: "linear" | "dag" | "generic"
- EscalationStrategy: "blocker" | "skip" | "abort"
- TransitionCondition: "success" | "failure" | "always" | "timeout"
- PhaseDefinition Schema (id, name, optional description, optional allowedTools[], optional maxRetries, optional escalation, optional fanOut, optional subagentTypes[])
- TransitionDefinition Schema (from, to, condition)
- LifecycleDefinition Schema (type, phases[], optional transitions[])
- PhaseResult interface (phase, status, error?, retriesUsed)
- parseLifecycle helper to safely parse unknown → LifecycleDefinition | undefined

### 2. builtins.ts — Built-in lifecycle constants
- CARTOGRAPHER_LIFECYCLE: dag, 3 phases (discover, map, synthesize) with allowedTools per phase
- EXECUTOR_LIFECYCLE: linear, 3 phases (read_plan, edit, verify) with allowedTools per phase
- CRITIC_LIFECYCLE: dag, 3 phases (read_plan, analyze, verdict) with allowedTools per phase
- LANE_OWNER_LIFECYCLE: linear, 5 phases (cartography, plan, review, execution, validation) with allowedTools per phase
- BUILTIN_LIFECYCLES: Record<string, LifecycleDefinition> mapping agent type names to lifecycles

### 3. engine.ts — LifecycleEngine Effect Service
- Interface { execute(input): Effect<PhaseResult[]> }
- Algorithm:
  1. Iterate phases by index
  2. Call onPhaseEnter hook
  3. Enter retry loop (up to maxRetries+1):
     a. Call onProcessorRun
     b. If success: break retry loop
     c. If failure + retries remain: call onRepair hook
     d. If maxRetries exceeded: apply escalation (abort returns immediately, skip continues, blocker stops)
  4. Call onPhaseExit hook
  5. Determine next phase:
     - linear: next index
     - dag: find transition matching condition
  6. If no next phase, stop

### 4. loop.ts — LifecycleLoop compositor
- Wraps SessionPrompt.loop with lifecycle phase management
- Uses Layer-level ToolRegistry override to filter tools by phase
- For each phase:
  1. Create a filtered ToolRegistry wrapping the real one
  2. Create a new SessionPrompt layer with filtered registry via Layer.provideMerge
  3. Run promptSvc.loop({ sessionID }) with the filtered layer
  4. Get the assistant message from the result
  5. Determine next phase based on finish reason / lifecycle transitions
- Exposes: runLifecycledPrompt() Effect function

### 5. lifecycle.ts — Barrel module
- export * from "./definition"
- export * from "./builtins"
- export * from "./engine"
- export * from "./loop"

## No modifications to dirty files
- prompt.ts (DIRTY) — not modified
- processor.ts (DIRTY) — not modified
- registry.ts (DIRTY) — not modified
- agent.ts (DIRTY) — already has lifecycle field
